instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Claim Settlement Ratio
The claim settlement ratio is an important metric that helps evaluate an insurance company’s credibility be reflecting its claim resolution history.
The claims paid ratio or the claim settlement ratio is computed by dividing the total number of claims settled by an insurer by the total number of claims received by the insurer in a given year.
In order to receive the payout from the policy in case of an unfortunate event, your beneficiaries will need to raise a claim with the insurance company from whom you have purchased the plan. The insurer will evaluate the claim application and then pay out the sum assured in case the claim is genuine and policy was in force, when the said event occurred. This process forms the basis of the claim settlement ratio of an insurer.
What Is a Claim Settlement Ratio?
The Claim Settlement Ratio, compiled and published annually by the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI), is the percentage of total claims an insurance company settles compared to the total number of claims it received in a given financial year. It is calculated by dividing the number of claims settled by the total number of claims filed (excluding claims rejected and those still pending) and multiplying the result by 100.
When choosing an insurer, a high term insurance claim settlement ratio can give you the confidence that the company is more likely to process your nominee’s claim in your absence.
Why Is Claim Settlement Ratio Important
The Claim Settlement Ratio is an important parameter when comparing insurance companies. Here are some reasons why –
01 Fosters trust in insurance company
A company attains a high Claim Settlement Ratio by settling its claims. This shows that the company is reliable in times of claim and increases the claim settlement probability for policyholders. This fosters trust among policyholders.
02 Promotes transparency
The Claim Settlement Ratio is published every year. This gives transparency to policyholders as they can review the claim settlement history of insurance companies and choose the right company.
03 Shows credibility
An insurance company maintaining a consistently high Claim Settlement Ratio is seen as a credible company which honours most of its claims. This also increases the reputation and goodwill of the company and can make it a preferred choice among customers.
04 Shows customer-first approach
A company having a high Claim Settlement Ratio can be seen as one that keeps its customers first which is why it settles their claims easily. This makes prospective policyholders more likely to choose the insurer over others.
How Is the Term Insurance Claim Settlement Ratio Calculated?
The term insurance claim settlement ratio is calculated using a simple formula as follows –
Claim Settlement Ratio (CSR) = (total number of claims paid in a given year / total number of claims raised in the year) X 100
For instance, in a financial year, if 100 claims are raised on the insurance company out of which the company has paid 95 claims, the term plan claim settlement ratio would be calculated as – (95 / 100) X 100 = 95%
Documents Required for Claim Settlement
In addition to checking the insurer’s claim settlement ratio, understanding what is required while filling makes the process smoother and increases the chances of a timely payout.
Mandatory Documents
Death claim form with NEFT details
Death certificate issued by local authorities
Original policy document (if applicable)
Claimant’s PAN card
Identity and address proof (such as Aadhaar, Passport or Voter ID)
Cancelled cheque or bank passbook showing name, account number and IFSC code
Additional Documents by Claim Type
Natural Death: Medical cause of death certificate and hospital records.
Accidental or Unnatural Death: FIR, post-mortem report, panchnama and police report.
Critical Illness: Claim form, diagnostic test reports and medical records.
Natural Calamities: Death certificate, claim form, PAN, address proof and bank details.
Note that the list of required documents may vary among insurers. It is therefore advisable to check with the insurer for more information.
Claim Settlement Process
The claim settlement process of a death claim under a term insurance plan is as follows –
Factors Influencing Claim Settlement Ratio Plain
Some of the factors that may affect the Claim Settlement Ratio of insurance companies are as follows
Underwriting practices
Insurance companies that follow strict underwriting practices issue the policy knowing the expected risks. This reduces the chances of fraud and claims are settled easily. The correctness of claim settlements means a higher Claim Settlement Ratio by reducing the likelihood of future fraudulent claims.
Claim process
Insurance companies having a simple claim process settle their claims quickly. This is because policyholders have to follow simple steps for claim settlement, and their payout may be credited promptly.
Customer service
Insurance companies with a dedicated customer care platform to handle claim-related queries can settle claims quickly. Open communication can lead to a better customer experience and favourably impact the Claim Settlement Ratio.
Types of Insurance Claims
There are various types of insurance claims, each corresponding to the specific event covered by the policy.
Maturity Claims
A maturity claim arises when a life assured survives the entire term of the endowment plan. This claim type represents the accumulated policy benefit paid out upon the policy’s specified date of maturity.
Death Claims
The nominee files a death claim upon the demise of the life assured. This is the core benefit of a life insurance policy, providing the predetermined sum assured to the surviving beneficiaries.
Common Reasons for Life Insurance Claim Rejection
The reliability of an insurance company, often measured by its Claim Settlement Ratio, is only meaningful to you if your individual claim is settled successfully. To maximize your chances of a hassle-free and swift claim payment, it is important to know these common reasons for claim rejection:
Incorrect Information
Providing accurate and consistent information is essential from the proposal stage to claim submission. Even minor errors, such as spelling mistakes, mismatched nominee details or incorrect personal information, can delay or lead to claim rejection. Discrepancies between documents, such as Aadhaar, PAN and the policy form, can create verification issues. Hiding lifestyle habits such as smoking or alcohol consumption, or not updating changes in nominee details, may also affect the validity of the claim. Clear, correct and updated documentation results in a smoother settlement process.
Unpaid Premiums
A policy remains active only when premiums are paid regularly and on or before the due date. Missing payments beyond the grace period causes the policy to lapse. In such cases, insurers are not obligated to honor any claims made during the inactive period. While there are options for revival, they must be utilized promptly. Regular and timely premium payments keep the policy in force, secure continuous protection for loved ones, and enable seamless claim settlement when required.
Incomplete or Misrepresented Medical Details
Life insurance applications require full disclosure of medical history and lifestyle details such as existing illnesses, surgeries or use of tobacco and alcohol. These details enable insurers to assess risk and the appropriate premiums. Hiding or misrepresenting such information can cause claim rejection if discovered during later verification. Transparency at the time of purchase encourages fairness and trust between the policyholder and insurer, thereby safeguarding beneficiaries from disputes over claims.
Undisclosed Risky Occupation or Hobby
Your occupations and hobbies may also influence risk assessment. Individuals engaged in high-risk activities, such as aviation, mining, firefighting or adventure sports, must accurately disclose these details for their safety. Failure to mention such activities may result in claim denial (or at least complications in the process) if the death is linked to these risks. Honest disclosure allows insurers to assess exposure and price the policy. It prevents future complications and guarantees that the nominees receive rightful benefits without delays.
Claims Falling Under Policy Exclusions
Every life insurance policy includes specific exclusions, such as suicide within 12 months of policy inception, deaths caused by illegal acts or those arising from war or terrorism. Claims falling under these exclusions are not payable, regardless of premium status. Policyholders must understand and clearly communicate these exclusions to their nominees at the time of purchase.
Note: The reasons listed above cover common causes for claim rejection, but are not exhaustive. Policy terms and conditions may vary between insurers. Always review your policy document carefully and maintain transparent communication with your insurer to avoid future complications.
How to Choose a Life Insurance Company in India with the Best Claim Settlement Ratio
Weigh in the following factors when selecting a life insurance company in India:
Claim Settlement Ratio
Refer to the annual report published by the IRDAI to discover insurers with a higher claim settlement ratio. A ratio above 95% may reflect reliability in paying genuine claims.
Customer Support
Look for an insurer that has responsive and 24/7 customer support. Excellent service will allow your nominee to receive assistance without any added stress during the claim process.
Customer Satisfaction
Review customer feedback to evaluate the company’s service quality and responsiveness during the claims processing process.
Inflation Protection
Select an insurer that offers adequate coverage options with value that maintains its worth against inflation, without incurring high premiums.
Riders and Add-ons
Prefer insurers that offer useful riders, such as critical illness coverage, accidental death benefits or waiver of premium, to enhance protection.
Evaluate Claim Repudiation Rate
Alongside the claim settlement ratio, look at the rejection percentage. A low rejection rate confirms the company's commitment to honouring valid claims.
Key Takeaways
Look for an insurer with a consistently high claim settlement ratio as this is the best indicator that the nominee will receive the payout smoothly and reliably.
Misrepresentation of facts is the primary reason for claim rejection, which lowers the claim settlement ratio for the entire industry.
For a quick claim, the nominee must submit all mandatory paperwork accurately to the insurer for a hassle-free settlement.
Conclusion
The goal of purchasing life insurance is to achieve financial security. When deciding, the most necessary metric to verify is the term insurance claim settlement ratio. Selecting an insurer with a consistently higher ratio assures that your loved ones will receive the promised payout quickly and without complications. This validates the entire purpose of your policy investment.
Frequently Asked Questions
1. What should one do to ensure a smooth claim settlement process for my term insurance plan?
To ensure a smooth claim settlement, inform the insurance company of the death timely. Moreover, submit all the relevant documents with the claim form. If any document is missing, the insurance company will inform you of the same. Get the missing document and submit it as soon as possible for quick claim settlements.
2. Where can I get claim form required for claim submission?
You can get the claim form by:
Downloading from the claims section on the insurer’s website
Visiting the nearest company branch
Contacting your Agent or insurance intermediary.
Disclaimer
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajlifeinsurance.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
